name: architecture-patterns
description: Organizational coding standards and architectural patterns. References comprehensive skills for detailed patterns. Use when making architecture decisions or implementing features.
capabilities: Guide pattern selection with decision trees
Reference Next.js Server and Client Components
Define database patterns with Drizzle ORM
Outline API design and validation patterns
author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing pattern references and decision trees. Contains only markdown files with code examples. Allowed tools are Read and Grep only. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or file system access beyond reading its own files.
valueStatement: Developers need consistent patterns when building applications. This skill provides decision trees and pattern references for Next.js, TypeScript, databases, APIs, and React 19. Choose the right pattern for each situation.
limitations: Contains pattern references, not full implementations
Links to comprehensive skills for details
Examples are templates, not production code
No code execution or file modifications

useCases.title: Choose component type
useCases.description: Decide between Server and Client Components based on interactivity needs

useCases.title: Design API endpoints
useCases.description: Structure REST APIs with authentication, validation, and error handling

useCases.title: Apply React 19 patterns
useCases.description: Use use(), useOptimistic, and Server Actions for modern React development

promptTemplates.title: Component type decision
promptTemplates.prompt: Should I use a Server Component or Client Component for a page that displays database data with a delete button?

promptTemplates.title: Database query pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the correct Drizzle ORM pattern for inserting a record with relations?

promptTemplates.title: API validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the Zod schema and validation pattern for a POST /api/users endpoint

promptTemplates.title: State management
promptTemplates.prompt: Should I use React Query, useState, or Context for storing user authentication state?

bestPractices: Prefer Server Components for data fetching to reduce client bundle size
Use Zod schemas for input validation at API boundaries
Apply type guards and explicit types for TypeScript strict mode
Choose state solution based on data source: server, local UI, or shared UI
antiPatterns: Using useState for server data instead of React Query or Server Components
Using string concatenation for SQL queries instead of parameterized queries
Using @ts-ignore instead of fixing underlying type errors
Fetching data in useEffect when Server Component data fetching would work

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Works as a pattern reference guide.

faq.question: What patterns does this skill cover?
faq.answer: Covers Next.js, TypeScript, Drizzle ORM, API design, React 19, and state management patterns.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. This is a pattern index that provides guidance. Use comprehensive skills for full implementations.

faq.question: How is data handled?
faq.answer: This skill only reads and references its own markdown files. No user data is collected or transmitted.

faq.question: Why reference other skills?
faq.answer: Pattern files contain decision trees and quick references. Comprehensive skills contain full implementation details.

faq.question: How does this compare to other pattern skills?
faq.answer: Acts as an index that references 6 comprehensive skills. Use this first, then deep dive into specific skills.
